sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 8.22 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.8)

statement.latex: If$G$is a (non-abelian) finite group contained in a join${ A} { B}$of two varieties${ A}, { B}$of groups, must there exist finite groups$A { A}$,$B { B}$such that$G$is a section of the direct product$A B$? { L.\,G.\,Kov\'acs} Not always (A.\,Storozhev, Bull. Austral. Math. Soc., { 51}, no.\,2 (1995), 287--290).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
